segments.text: Listen to this, you descendants of Jacob, you who are called by the name of Israel and come from the line of Judah, you who take oaths in the name of the Lord and invoke the God of Israel—
but not in truth or righteousness—
you who call yourselves citizens of the holy city and claim to rely on the God of Israel—
the Lord Almighty is his name:
I foretold the former things long ago, my mouth announced them and I made them known; then suddenly I acted, and they came to pass.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I knew how stubborn you were; your neck muscles were iron, your forehead was bronze.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore I told you these things long ago; before they happened I announced them to you so that you could not say, 'My images brought them about; my wooden image and metal god ordained them.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You have heard these things; look at them all. Will you not admit them? From now on I will tell you of new things, of hidden things unknown to you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They are created now, and not long ago; you have not heard of them before today. So you cannot say, 'Yes, I knew of them.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You have neither heard nor understood; from of old your ears have not been open. Well do I know how treacherous you are; you were called a rebel from birth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For my own name's sake I delay my wrath; for the sake of my praise I hold it back from you, so as not to destroy you completely.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For my own sake, for my own sake, I do this. How can I let myself be defamed? I will not yield my glory to another.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Listen to me, Jacob, Israel, whom I have called: I am he; I am the first and I am the last.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: My own hand laid the foundations of the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens; when I summon them, they all stand up together.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Come together, all of you, and listen: Which of the idols has foretold these things? The Lord's chosen ally will carry out his purpose against Babylon; his arm will be against the Babylonians.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I, even I, have spoken; yes, I have called him. I will bring him, and he will succeed in his mission.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Come near me and listen to this: From the first announcement I have not spoken in secret; at the time it happens, I am there.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And now the Sovereign Lord has sent me, endowed with his Spirit.
This is what the Lord says— your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you what is best for you, who directs you in the way you should go.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If only you had paid attention to my commands, your peace would have been like a river, your well-being like the waves of the sea.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Your descendants would have been like the sand, your children like its numberless grains; their name would never be blotted out nor destroyed from before me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Leave Babylon, flee from the Babylonians!
Announce this with shouts of joy and proclaim it. Send it out to the ends of the earth;
say, 'The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob.'
They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts; he made water flow for them from the rock; he split the rock and water gushed out.
There is no peace for the wicked.
segments.speaker: God
